RESPONDENT NTT DATA SERVICES, LLC A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF-APPELLANT DANIELLE CHEEK TO ANSWER REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS (TENTATIVE RULING PART 1 OF 2)
Off Calendar Feb-03-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff-Appellant Danielle Cheek To Answer Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-03-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Plaintiff / Appelant DANIELLE CHEEK, in pro per present (tel: 415-470-2274).
Atty. SUSAN YE, Esq. of Littler Mendelson, P.C. (tel: 415-433-1940) for Defendant NTT Data Services, LLC and Dell Inc.

Hearing Re: RESPONDENT NTT DATA SERVICES, LLC's Motion To Compel Plaintiff-Appellant Danielle Cheek To Answer Request For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Request For Sanctions.

Matter argued as to the deadline for written responses and production of documents. Upon conclusion, the Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications:

The motion to compel request for production responses and for sanctions is granted.

Defendant NTT Data Services LLC served written requests for production on plaintiff Danielle Cheek. A party to whom such a discovery request is made is obliged to respond unless the party obtains a protective order. CCP 2031.210(a). Cheek has not responded. The fact that she is out of state does not excuse her lack of response. The fact that NTT previously had an opportunity to view documents that are now being requested does not relieve Cheek of the obligation to respond. Having failed to respond, Cheek has waived objections to the requests for production. (CCP 2031.300(a).) The court understands that Cheek is representing herself, but Cheek is obliged nonetheless to comply with civil procedure rules. Moreover, Cheek appears to be asserting that she will return for trial in this matter but because she is out of state until then she has no obligation to comply with pretrial procedural requirements. That proposition is not supported by the law and would not accord procedural fairness to both sides.

Cheek is to produce responsive documents by February 7, 2025. Cheek is also ordered to respond to the written requests for production without objection by February 14, 2025.

NTT also requests sanctions. CCP 2031.300(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to a demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling, unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The court finds that Cheek did not act with substantial justification here but also finds it would be unjust to award the full amount requested by NTT. Cheek is ordered to pay $100 in sanctions to NTT within 15 court days of notice of entry of this order.

The Court finds service was proper.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR# 13714 (tel: 415-601-7054 / email: apourtabib@gmail.com). (301/CVA)
